steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: The terms $8x^2$ and $6x^2$ are like terms because they have the same variable part, $x^2$. The term $8x$ has a different variable part, so it cannot be combined with the squared terms. This rules out choices that mix the $x$ and $x^2$ terms.

steps.title: Combine the coefficients
steps.content:
Add only the coefficients of the like terms, and leave the unlike term unchanged.
$$8x^2+6x^2+8x=(8+6)x^2+8x=14x^2+8x$$
The answer is D.
